title: quercus rostrata
intro: Quercus rostrata, also known as the Nelson spruce or Nelson fir, is a species of evergreen tree in the cypress family. Native to western North America, it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus rostrata include reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. The herbal preparations of Quercus rostrata often involve infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other medicinal products.
